summary:
• Iran's continued oil loading despite US blockade efforts creates supply uncertainty while diplomatic talks offer conflicting signals on potential sanctions relief that could significantly impact global crude markets.

• US domestic drillers are scaling back production amid a global supply crunch, reducing Western Hemisphere output at a time when geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are supporting elevated oil prices.

• Colombia's oil industry revival driven by higher crude prices and renewed investment interest represents a potential bright spot for Americas supply, though broader geopolitical risks in Iran and the Middle East could offset regional gains.
